purpose: Define evidence quality penalty candidates for research review and Decision Lab validation.

penalties.name: stale_information
penalties.description: Evidence is old relative to the market reaction window.

penalties.name: single_source
penalties.description: Evidence depends on only one source and lacks confirmation.

penalties.name: rumor_or_unverified
penalties.description: Evidence is rumor-based, uncited, or not independently verified.

penalties.name: conflicting_evidence
penalties.description: Important evidence contradicts the current interpretation.

penalties.name: narrative_overfit
penalties.description: Evidence mainly supports a popular story but lacks factual confirmation.

penalties.name: late_confirmation
penalties.description: Evidence appeared only after the market had already moved.

penalties.name: source_bias
penalties.description: Source may have incentive, positioning, or framing bias.

rules: Penalties must not directly change production scoring.
Penalty usage should be recorded in Decision Lab cases when evidence quality affects the outcome.
Any production use requires 小P review and a separate 小C implementation task.
